mechanism_narrative: A rise in the housing market signal, as indicated by a 2-month increase in HOUST, indicates a strengthening demand for goods and services, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in post-shock periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the unemployment rate signal, as indicated by a 3-month rise in UNRATE, indicates a weakening labor market, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.05 over any 1-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in late-cycle periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production signal, as indicated by a 2-month fall in INDPRO, indicates a weakening supply of goods and services, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in pre-shock periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the retail sales signal, as indicated by a 3-month increase in RSAFS, indicates a strengthening demand for goods and services, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the employment rate signal, as indicated by a 2-month rise in PAYEMS, indicates a strengthening labor market, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in post-shock periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the goods demand signal, as indicated by a 3-month fall in HOUST, indicates a weakening demand for goods and services, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the monetary policy signal, as indicated by a 2-month increase in FEDFUNDS, indicates an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the supply of goods signal, as indicated by a 3-month fall in INDPRO, indicates a weakening supply of goods and services, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in pre-shock periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
